text: The study reviews the situation of digital inclusion and access to information technologies in the rural areas of Ecuador, problems and possibilities that are exposed in the research work and that show the enormous challenges that rural areas still have to travel to achieve an integral digital inclusion that promotes social and economic development. In Ecuador the access to technology infrastructure in rural areas is limited, the survey indicates that only 27% of rural households in Ecuador access the Internet from some device, which represents a 33 percentage points less than that recorded for urban areas (67%). Factors such as inadequate technological devices, lack of network coverage, are part of the existing factors that limit access to the opportunities derived from the use of information technologies. In relation to the basic digital skills training programmes, the achievement results are mixed, the training of skills in the adequate and pertinent use of technology are more effective when the training includes a technological component and when it is a continuous activity, in this sense, it has been verified that the programmes that have a better impact for the people that participate, have a technological component complemented with a process of accompaniment that, in various moments, is carried out through different systems of communication. As for the public policies that support access, the programme “Ecuador Digital” is one of the main initiatives of the State that intends to carry out the modernization of the access infrastructure, with results that, in general, have demonstrated the functionality of the infrastructure built, but which present great limitations in relation to the coverage of the towns and at the level of sustainability. On the other hand, it is important to highlight the important contribution of civil society organizations that through diverse activities and programs have enhanced the access to digital technologies, the educational processes carried out in the classrooms and the appropriation of the technology through the access to the devices. Limitations To be able to have access to and benefit from the opportunities derived from the use of ICT, there are numerous limitations such as the lack of financial resources that prevent access to the technology, the deep-rooted social and cultural biases that generate distrust for the adoption of a new technology that, in certain cases, do not understand why it is important and how it can contribute to improve the living conditions, as they think that they already have all that is necessary to carry out their daily tasks. Alternatives It is possible to use the mobile phones for a greater number of functions, the existing applications that could be copied to the context of the rural zones allow access to information that is not currently available, thanks to networks and systems that allow the use of technologies from anywhere, thus contributing to a greater number of opportunities to reach the population that is located away from the city centres. Recommendations To continue working on increasing the investments that are made on the access infrastructure in the rural zones. To strengthen the public-private policies in such a way that there is a high participation of private companies, to develop the appropriate training on the pertinent and adequate use of the technology for each level of knowledge. To carry out a permanent assessment of all the policies that are implemented to achieve an integral digital inclusion.
explanation: The passage is coherent and reads like a research summary, which is common in AI-generated texts; however, a numerical inconsistency (urban 67% vs rural 27% implying a 40-point gap, not 33) hints at possible human error or imperfect generation.
